title: Production publish: static site + printable packs + versioning/staleness + disclaimers
context: The production publish is the final technical step before partner distribution. It must deploy a static build over HTTPS, generate printable packs, implement the validUntil staleness fail-safe (which withholds HIGH-risk units past their review window until re-signed-off), display visible disclaimers and attribution, and pass the no-telemetry/no-PII audit. Depends on WCAG 2.2 AA hardening (a11y-013) and the i18n framework (i18n-019) being complete.
objective: Deploy the wash-guides static site to production (HTTPS), generate printable content packs, implement the validUntil staleness fail-safe that withholds expired HIGH-risk units until re-verified-and-signed-off, and confirm the 'not an official/endorsed product' disclaimer and attribution are visible on every page; verify no telemetry/PII at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Static build is deployed over HTTPS to a production host (e.g., GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Cloudflare Pages); build artifacts are reproducible
Printable packs are generated for each content unit and locale, including all required attribution, citations, and disclaimers in the printed version

The validUntil staleness fail-safe is implemented: any HIGH-risk unit past its validUntil date is withheld from the published site until re-verified and re-signed-off; a staleness warning is shown for medium-risk units past their window
Every page/pack displays a visible 'not an official or endorsed WHO/UNICEF/CDC product' notice and full attribution and citation list
No telemetry endpoints, third-party scripts, cookies (beyond offline renderer minimum), analytics, or PII fields are present; verified by static audit and runtime no-egress check
Reviewed and approved by a maintainer and the project steward (once secured) distinct from the author; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint all pass
output: A production-deployed static site with printable packs, staleness fail-safe, required disclaimers and attribution, and verified no-telemetry/no-PII posture.
